articles.title: Robodog row: Galgotias out of AI Summit
articles.description: Galgotias University faced backlash at the AI Impact Summit for misrepresenting exhibits, leading to its removal for misleading claims about technology.| India News
articles.content:
The exhibitor, Galgotias University was asked and then forced to vacate its stall at the expo on Wednesday by the government.
“We want genuine exhibitors at the expo. We don’t want controversy around exhibits here. Plagiarism, misinformation cannot b... [2649 chars]

articles.title: Marut Drones: Engineering utility into every take-off
articles.description: CE speaks to Prem Kumar Vislawath, founder of Marut Drones Pvt Ltd, on building purpose-driven drone technology that empowers farmers, creates rural livelihoods
articles.content:

Tell us about the inspiration that led to the creation of Marut Drones in 2019?
We were trying to solve a problem. At Marut, we were trying to identify the problem statement around dengue and malaria. We were using drones to eradicate dengue... [8417 chars]

articles.title: AI is offering Global South a unique opportunity to catch up fast: Microsoft’s Brad Smith
articles.description: Brad Smith, Microsoft’s vice-chair, said AI gives the Global South a chance for “catch-up growth” as technology adoption and investment increase. He called India a “land of digital opportunity” and highlighted the company’s $17 billion-plus investment supporting the country’s $200 billion AI infrastructure push. Opportunities exist across infrastructure, platforms, and applications.
articles.content:
Synopsis

articles.title: Rep. Fry to Newsmax: Tech Is Outpacing Washington Laws
articles.description: Rep. Russell Fry, R-S.C., told Newsmax that parents are concerned that technology is outpacing Washington, D.C., laws and the regulatory environment, arguing that any response to teen social media use should involve families in policy decisions.
articles.content: Rep. Russell Fry, R-S.C., told Newsmax on Wednesday that parents are concerned that "technology is outpacing Washington, D.C., laws and the regulatory environment," arguing that any response to teen social media use should involve families in policy ... [3401 chars]

articles.title: North Korea's Strategic Ambitions: A New Era of Military Might
articles.description: North Korean leader Kim Jong Un aims to unveil new military and construction goals at the upcoming Workers' Party Congress. Gifted with advanced rocket launchers, Kim envisions strategic military enhancements incorporating AI technology. The Congress will spotlight self-reliant defense initiatives amidst potential leadership shifts.
articles.content:
North Korean leader Kim Jong Un is poised to announce new military and construction objectives during an upcoming crucial party congress, as reported by state media KCNA.
On Wednesday, Kim marked a ceremony where munitions workers gifted 600mm-calibr... [557 chars]

articles.title: Amid spending cuts and pending charges, McIver brings Newark home $1M for STEM
articles.description: On Tuesday, the congresswoman announced a $1 million appropriation she had secured for an science, technology, engineering and mathematics afterschool program.
articles.content:
U.S. Rep. LaMonica McIver showed Newark constituents she can still deliver federal funding even amid a pending indictment and spending cuts by the Trump Administration.
McIver, a Democrat who represents New Jersey’s 10th Congressional District, visit... [1922 chars]

articles.title: LG Ladakh visits several technology pavilions at Bharat Mandapam
articles.description: Excelsior Correspondent 19NEW DELHI, Feb 18: As India advances decisively on the path of becoming a global technology leader, Artificial Intelligence is emerging as a key enabler of inclusive growth, good governance and sustainable development. This was stated by the Lieutenant Governor of Ladakh, Kavinder Gupta, during his visit to the India AI Summit being held at Bharat Mandapam, New Delhi. During his visit, the LG visited several technology pavilions and interacted with representatives of leading global and Indian companies, […]

articles.title: Deep-sea tech deployed to find Lily Jean
articles.description: GLOUCESTER — A coalition of authorities is deploying technology to try to locate the wreck of a fishing boat that sank last month off Massachusetts, killing all seven aboard. But winter weather and sea conditions have thus far slowed their efforts. The 72-foot vessel Lily Jean was returning to port early Jan. 30 to repair […]
